Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated serum creatinine), hyperchloremia, and significant proteinuria with ketonuria and glucosuria, suggesting possible renal tubular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, raising suspicion for urinary tract pathology despite negative nitrites/leukocytes. Recommend further renal and urinary tract evaluation and clinical correlation for possible infection or metabolic derangement.
